name: first_n_nat_finite
reason: The set `{0, ..., n-1}` is always finite — direct consequence of the verified lemma

name: first_zero_empty

name: first_n_contains_pred
code:
proof fn phi_3_first_n_contains_pred(n: nat)
    requires
        n > 0,
    ensures
        Set::new(|i: nat| i < n).contains((n - 1) as nat),
{
}
reason: The set `{0, ..., n-1}` contains `n-1` — tests membership at the upper boundary

name: first_n_not_contains_n
reason: The set `{0, ..., n-1}` does not contain `n` — tests the strict upper bound

name: first_n_insert_equals_first_n_plus_one
reason: Inserting `n` into `{0,...,n-1}` equals `{0,...,n}` — tests the recursive construction used in the proof
